question: In the CMS flow, where should a user with existing sites go right after login?

options.text: Directly to the manage websites dashboard showing created site snapshots.
options.feedback: Correct: returning users should resume management from their existing sites dashboard.

options.text: Back to the marketing home page to read product presentation again.
options.feedback: Post-login flow should prioritize task continuation, not repeated landing content.

options.text: Immediately into a forced new-site wizard for every login session.
options.feedback: Forcing creation ignores existing assets and adds unnecessary workflow friction.

options.text: A generic account settings page with no website context.
options.feedback: Settings-first navigation delays core objective of managing current websites.

whyQuestion: Why is direct dashboard routing better for users with existing websites?

whyOptions.text: It reduces friction and moves users immediately to relevant management actions.
whyOptions.feedback: Exactly: contextual routing shortens time-to-task and improves product usability.

whyOptions.text: It prevents users from creating any additional sites after first login.
whyOptions.feedback: The goal is efficient access, not restricting future site creation.

whyOptions.text: It is required because CMS products cannot render public landing pages.
whyOptions.feedback: Landing pages remain useful; routing choice depends on authenticated context.

whyOptions.text: It removes the need for per-site dashboards and blog management screens.
whyOptions.feedback: Per-site dashboards remain essential after selecting a specific website.
